claimText: The Islands Protection Zone (IPZ) was notified in 2011 under the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986.
evidenceExcerpt: IPZ was notified in 2011 under the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986
examUse: Statement-based question on the statutory backing of the IPZ notification.

claimText: The Islands Protection Zone (IPZ) framework is specifically designed to safeguard the ecological integrity of the Andaman & Nicobar and Lakshadweep groups of islands.
evidenceExcerpt: IPZ is a regulatory framework to safeguard the ecological integrity of Andaman & Nicobar and Lakshadweep groups of islands.
examUse: Statement-based question on the geographical applicability of the IPZ.
distractorHooks: Applies to all coastal islands of India
Applies to inland river islands like Majuli

claimText: The IPZ regulation includes the Island Coastal Regulation Zone (ICRZ) and Integrated Islands Management Plans (IIMPs).
evidenceExcerpt: The IPZ regulation includes the Island Coastal Regulation Zone (ICRZ) and Integrated Islands Management Plans (IIMPs).
examUse: Statement-based question identifying the specific regulatory components of the IPZ.

claimText: The TN-SHORE initiative is a regional coastal conservation project in Tamil Nadu that is largely funded by the World Bank.
evidenceExcerpt: TN-SHORE is a regional initiative largely funded by the World Bank to strengthen Tamil Nadu's coastal economy and resilience.
examUse: Correct-pair question matching regional conservation initiatives with their funding agencies.
distractorHooks: Funded by the Asian Development Bank (ADB)
Funded by the Global Environment Facility (GEF)
